activities_projects.title: Population Distribution Map Activity
activities_projects.objective: Understand uneven population distribution in India
steps: Locate Uttar Pradesh, Maharashtra, Bihar and West Bengal on the map
Shade these states to show high population
Write reasons for high population in these regions
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct map marking and explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Population Density Comparison
steps: Write the population density of India
Find the density of Bihar and Arunachal Pradesh
Compare and explain the difference
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand variations in population density

activities_projects.title: Census Data Study
activities_projects.objective: Learn how census provides population information
steps: Write the meaning of census
Note when the first census in India was conducted
Explain why census data is important
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the importance of census data

activities_projects.title: Population Growth Graph
steps: Collect population data for India from 1951 to 2011
Plot the data on graph paper
Observe and write two conclusions from the graph
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students analyze population growth trends using graphs
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of graph and conclusions (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Birth Rate and Death Rate Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Understand the relationship between birth rate and death rate
steps: Write definitions of birth rate and death rate
Explain why birth rates were higher in the past
Discuss how declining death rates affected population growth

activities_projects.title: Migration Causes Activity
activities_projects.objective: Identify push and pull factors of migration
steps: List three push factors in rural areas
List three pull factors of cities
Write a short paragraph explaining rural-urban migration

activities_projects.title: Urban Population Study
steps: Write the percentage of urban population in 1951 and 2011
Explain reasons for urban population increase
Give examples of large cities

activities_projects.title: Adolescent Population Discussion
activities_projects.objective: Understand the importance of adolescent population
steps: Write the age group of adolescents
Discuss why adolescents are important for the future
Mention two health challenges faced by adolescents
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students recognize the significance of adolescent population

activities_projects.title: Population Policy Study
steps: Write the meaning of population policy
List three objectives of the National Population Policy 2000
Explain how it helps control population growth

activities_projects.title: Local Population Survey
activities_projects.objective: Learn data collection through simple survey
steps: Survey five families in your neighborhood
Record the number of members in each family
Prepare a small table and calculate the average family size
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn basic demographic data collection
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct data recording and calculation (10 marks)
